back: A pattern used to match and manipulate text, enabling sophisticated text searching without complex parsing.
Searches for first match of a pattern in text, returns match object or None.

front: What do these regex symbols mean: ., *, +, ??
back: . any char, * 0+ occurrences, + 1+ occurrences, ? 0 or 1 occurrence.

front: What are character classes [] used for?
back: Match any single character inside brackets, e.g., [aeiou] matches any vowel.
Replaces all matches of a pattern with a replacement string.
